money_flow_summary: Money flows from patients → insurers → providers. Solo opportunity: health administration automation and wellness tools where AI delivers value without FDA approval.

opportunities.title: AI Medical Scribe for Private Practice
opportunities.how_money_is_made: Ambient AI scribe listens to doctor-patient conversations, generates structured SOAP notes, exports to EHR. Charge $199–$499/mo per provider. Doctors spend 2+ hrs/day on documentation.
opportunities.moat: EHR integration complexity; doctor workflow familiarity creates stickiness
opportunities.first_step: Mobile audio capture + Whisper + Claude SOAP note; integrate AthenaHealth API; get 5 beta doctors
opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI listens to natural conversation and produces structured clinical documentation without doctor touching keyboard

competitors.name: Suki AI
competitors.weakness: $249–$349/mo; limited EHR integrations; voice-command based not ambient; poor accuracy on specialty terms

competitors.name: Nuance DAX (Microsoft)
competitors.weakness: Enterprise contracts; $500+/provider/mo; long implementation; requires IT department

competitors.name: Freed AI
competitors.weakness: New entrant; basic note taking; no EHR push integration; limited formatting

opportunities.gap: Suki is $249/mo but requires the doctor to speak commands. A truly ambient scribe that listens passively and produces structured notes ready to push to Epic/AthenaHealth is missing at the SMB ($199/mo) price point.
poc_steps: Build a simple iOS app that records audio
Send recording to Whisper API for transcription
Feed transcript to Claude with SOAP note prompt
Show a doctor friend the output — compare to what they'd write manually

opportunities.title: Prior Authorization AI Agent for Medical Practices
opportunities.how_money_is_made: AI reads insurance requirements, gathers EHR documentation, fills PA forms, submits electronically, tracks status. Charge $299–$999/mo. A primary care practice spends $15k–$30k/yr on prior auth.
opportunities.moat: Insurance plan requirement database grows over time; EHR integrations create high switching cost
opportunities.first_step: Build a scraper for CMS prior auth requirements; integrate with AthenaHealth patient data; demo with 1 practice doing 20+ PAs/week
opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI reads unstructured insurance documents, matches to patient clinical data, fills complex forms autonomously

competitors.name: Availity
competitors.weakness: Clearinghouse, not automation — still requires staff to fill forms; free but no AI

competitors.name: Olive AI (shut down 2023)
competitors.weakness: Failed — proves this is hard but demand is real; enterprise only pricing was the problem

competitors.name: Cohere Health
competitors.weakness: Enterprise only; $100k+/yr; requires insurance company integration

opportunities.gap: Olive AI failed because they targeted large health systems at $1M+ ACV. Solo/small practices (50k+ in the US) have the same pain and no affordable solution. $299–$999/mo for practice-level PA automation is completely unaddressed.
poc_steps: Pick 1 insurance company's PA requirements for 1 common procedure (e.g., MRI)
Build form-fill bot using Playwright for the payer portal
Test with 5 real PA submissions from 1 practice (with consent)
